subject: Chemistry
q: In a binary compound, atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> form a hcp structure and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></math> of the tetrahedral voids of the hcp structure. The formula of the binary compound is:
question: In a binary compound, atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> form a hcp structure and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></math> of the tetrahedral voids of the hcp structure. The formula of the binary compound is:
solution:
In HCP unit cell number of effective number of atoms is 6,

so  A=6.

Number of tetrahedral voids

                                  = 2 x number of atoms forming the lattice 

                                  =2 X 6

M12×23 A6
M8 A6
M4 A3

subject: Chemistry
q: The correct relationships between unit cell edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’ and radius of sphere ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math>’ for face-centred and body-centred cubic structures respectively are:
question: The correct relationships between unit cell edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’ and radius of sphere ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math>’ for face-centred and body-centred cubic structures respectively are:
solution:
A face-centered cubic (fcc) unit cell contains a component in the center of each face in addition to those at the corners of the cube. The atoms touch each other along the face diagonal.

For  FCC:  a2=4r

a=22r

A body-centered cubic (bcc) unit cell contains a component in the center of the cube in addition to those at the corners of the cube. The atoms touch each other along the body diagonal.

For BCC : a3=4r

subject: Chemistry
q: Lithium forms body centred cubic structure. The length of the side of its unit cell is $351 \mathrm{pm}$. Atomic radius of the lithium will be:
question: Lithium forms body centred cubic structure. The length of the side of its unit cell is $351 \mathrm{pm}$. Atomic radius of the lithium will be:
solution:
For BCC, $\sqrt{3} a=4 r$
$r=\frac{\sqrt{3} \times 351}{4}=152 \mathrm{~pm}$

subject: Chemistry
q: A compound is formed by two elements X and Y. The element Y forms cubic close packed arrangement and those of element X occupy one third of the tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound?
question: A compound is formed by two elements X and Y. The element Y forms cubic close packed arrangement and those of element X occupy one third of the tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound?
solution:
Atoms of Element Y, are present in cubic close packing. In cubic close packing the number of atoms per unit cell is 4.

So, the effective number of element Y, per unit cell is 4

Element X, are present at one third of the tetrahedral voids.

Number of tetrahedral void =Twice the number of atoms in unit cell

Number of atoms of Element X are present unit cell =13×8=83

X : Y83 : 42   : 3

Formula of the compound =X2Y3

question:
A certain element crystallises in a bcc lattice of unit cell edge length 27 Ao. If the same element under the same conditions crystallises in the fcc lattice, the edge length of the unit cell in Ao will be __________. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).

[Assume each lattice point has a single atom]

[Assume 3=1.73, 2=1.41]
solution:
For BCC 3a=4r

so r=34×27

for FCC  a=22r

=2×2×34×27

=32×27

=33

q: Sodium metal crystallizes in a body centred cubic lattice with a unit cell edge of <math><mn>4.29</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Å</mi></math> . The radius of sodium atom is approximately:
question: Sodium metal crystallizes in a body centred cubic lattice with a unit cell edge of <math><mn>4.29</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Å</mi></math> . The radius of sodium atom is approximately:
solution:
For B.C.C lattice,

4 r = 3 a

where 'r' is radius of atom and 'a' is edge length of unit cell

r = 3 4 a = 1.732 4 × 4.29

= 1.86 Å

q: Which of the following arrangements shows the schematic alignment of magnetic moments of antiferromagnetic substance?
question: Which of the following arrangements shows the schematic alignment of magnetic moments of antiferromagnetic substance?
solution: Substances which are expected to possess para-magnetic or ferromagnetism on the basis of unpaired electrons, but actually they possess zero net magnetic moment are called anti-ferromagnetic substance.

solution:
If number of lattice points are N.

then effective octahedral voids =N

So, octahedral voids / lattice site =1

q: Among the following the incorrect statement is
question: Among the following the incorrect statement is
options: Density of crystals remains unaffected due to Frenkel defect.
In BCC unit cell the void space is $32 \%$.
Density of crystals decreases due to Schottky defect.
Electrical conductivity of semiconductors and metals increases with increase in temperature.
solution: The electrical resistance of metals depends upon temperature. Electrical resistance decreases with decrease in temperature and becomes zero near the absolute temperature. Material in this state is said to possess super conductivity.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
solution: A Frenkel defect is another form of a point defect which is created when an atom or cation leaves its original place in the lattice structure to create a vacancy while occupying another interstitial position within the solid crystal. Frenkel defect is dislocation defect, it is not interstitial defect, and it is not responsible for colour of solid.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following expressions is correct in case of a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CsCl</mi></math> unit cell (edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’)<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>?</mo></math>
question: Which of the following expressions is correct in case of a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CsCl</mi></math> unit cell (edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’)<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>?</mo></math>
solution:
CsCl has body centered type structure in which Cs+ occupies at corner of a cube and Cl– occupies the centre of the cube.

2rCs++2rCl-=3a (where a is the edge length of the cube)

rCs++rCr=32a

subject: Chemistry
q: A compound of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> has the HCP lattice. Which atom forms the HCP lattice and what fraction of the tetrahedral voids are occupied by the other atoms?
question: A compound of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> has the HCP lattice. Which atom forms the HCP lattice and what fraction of the tetrahedral voids are occupied by the other atoms?
solution:
Atom B forms HCP as anions are larger in size.

From the formula A2B3,

Number of B atoms=3,

Then, number of tetrahedral voids =2×Tetrahedral voids=2×3=6.

As 'A' is occupying tetrahedral voids,

  1 3  of tetrahedral voids=13×6=2

Therefore, formula of the compound=A2B3

subject: Chemistry
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KBr</mi></math> is doped with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup></math> mole percent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SrBr</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> The number of cationic vacancies in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KBr</mi></math> crystal is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>14</mn></msup></math>. (Round off to the Nearest Integer). [Atomic Mass :  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>39</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>Br</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>79</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>9</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math>,<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open=""><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>023</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KBr</mi></math> is doped with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup></math> mole percent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SrBr</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> The number of cationic vacancies in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KBr</mi></math> crystal is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>14</mn></msup></math>. (Round off to the Nearest Integer). [Atomic Mass :  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>39</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>Br</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>79</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>9</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math>,<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open=""><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>023</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>
solution:
1 mole KBr (=119gm) have 10-5100 moles SrBr2 and hence, 10-7 moles cation vacancy (as 1Sr2+ will result 1 cation vacancy)

∴ Required number of cation vacancies

=10-7×6.023×1023119=5.06×1014≃5×1014

subject: Chemistry
q: Metal deficiency defect is shown by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>93</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>. In the crystal, some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> cations are missing and loss of positive charge is compensated by the presence of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions. The percentage of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions in the<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>93</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> crystals is____(Nearest integer)
question: Metal deficiency defect is shown by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>93</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>. In the crystal, some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> cations are missing and loss of positive charge is compensated by the presence of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions. The percentage of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions in the<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>93</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> crystals is____(Nearest integer)
solution:
In Fe0.93O for every 93Fe ions 14 are Fe+3 and 93-14=79 are Fe+2 ions

∴ %Fe+2=7993×100=84.9%

∴ nearest integer =85%

subject: Chemistry
q: An element crystallises in a face–centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge a. The distance between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is:
question: An element crystallises in a face–centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge a. The distance between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is:
solution:
In FCC octahedral voids are present at the edge centers and body center.

Minimum distance between centers of two octahedral voids

=x=a22+a22
=a24+a24=a2

subject: Chemistry
q:
Number of crystal systems from the following where body centred unit cell can be found, is _____.

Cubic, tetragonal, orthorhombic, hexagonal, rhombohedral, monoclinic, triclinic
question:
Number of crystal systems from the following where body centred unit cell can be found, is _____.

Cubic, tetragonal, orthorhombic, hexagonal, rhombohedral, monoclinic, triclinic
solution:
Bravais Lattice refers to the 14 different 3-dimensional configurations into which atoms can be arranged in crystals. The smallest group of symmetrically aligned atoms which can be repeated in an array to make up the entire crystal is called a unit cell. Crystal systems where body centred unit cell can be found are Cubic, orthorhombic and tetragonal

Hence, correct answer is 3

subject: Chemistry
q: In a solid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> atoms are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ccp</mi></math> arrangement and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> atoms occupy all the octahedral sites. If two atoms from the opposite faces are removed, then the resultant stoichiometry of the compound is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is [nearest integer]
question: In a solid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> atoms are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ccp</mi></math> arrangement and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> atoms occupy all the octahedral sites. If two atoms from the opposite faces are removed, then the resultant stoichiometry of the compound is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is [nearest integer]
solution:
A forms ccp structure that means number of A atoms per unit cell →4

B atoms per unit cell →4 (Octahedral voids), as number of octahedral voids will be equal to number of atoms per unit cell.

A removed =2×12=1

A left =4-1=3

Formula becomes A3B4

x=3.

q: In a compound atoms of element $Y$ from ccp lattice and those of element $X$ occupy $2 / 3^{\text {rd }}$ of tetrahedral voids. The formula of the compound will be
question: In a compound atoms of element $Y$ from ccp lattice and those of element $X$ occupy $2 / 3^{\text {rd }}$ of tetrahedral voids. The formula of the compound will be
solution:
No. of atoms of $Y=4$
No. of atoms of $X=\frac{2}{3} \times 8$
Formula of compound will be $X_4 Y_3$

subject: Chemistry
q:
A solid having density of $9 \times 10^{3} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{-3}$ forms face centred cubic crystals of edge length $200 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{pm} .$ What is the molar mass of the solid?

[Avogadro constant $\left.\approx 6 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, \pi \approx 3\right]$
question:
A solid having density of $9 \times 10^{3} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{-3}$ forms face centred cubic crystals of edge length $200 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{pm} .$ What is the molar mass of the solid?

[Avogadro constant $\left.\approx 6 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, \pi \approx 3\right]$
solution:
Density $=\frac{Z \times M}{N_{A} \times a^{3}}$

$9 \times 10^{3}=\frac{4 \times \mathrm{M}}{\left(200 \times \sqrt{2} \times 10^{-12}\right)^{3} 6 \times 10^{23}}$

subject: Chemistry
q: The appearance of colour in solid alkali metal halides is generally due to:
question: The appearance of colour in solid alkali metal halides is generally due to:
solution:
The appearance of colour in alkali metal halides is generally due to F-centres. F centers in $\mathrm{NaCl}$ produce a band of optical absorption towards the blue end of visible
spectrum, thus the colour appears yellow under transmitted light. On the other hand, $\mathrm{KCl}$ with $\mathrm{F}$-centers appears magenta, and $\mathrm{KBr}$ appears blue.

question: The unit cell of copper corresponds to a face centered cube of edge length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>596</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Å</mi></math> with one copper atom at each lattice point. The calculated density of copper in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>kg</mi><mo>/</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> is ___ . [Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Cu</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>63</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>54</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>;</mo></math> Avogadro Number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>022</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>]
solution:
FCC,

d=Z×MNA×a3=4×63.541000×6.022×1023×3.596×10-103

=9076 kg/m3

subject: Chemistry
q: Element <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> forms ccp structure and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> occupies half of the octahedral voids, while oxygen atoms occupy all the tetrahedral voids. The structure of bimetallic oxide is:
question: Element <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> forms ccp structure and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> occupies half of the octahedral voids, while oxygen atoms occupy all the tetrahedral voids. The structure of bimetallic oxide is:
solution:
For ccp structure:
Number of octahedral voids are =4
Number of tetrahedral voids are =8
ZB=4 (number of atoms present in ccp structure)
ZA=12×4=2
ZO=8
So formula is A2B4O8=AB2O4.

subject: Chemistry
q: Copper crystallises in fcc with a unit length of $361 \mathrm{~pm}$. What is the radius of copper atom ?
question: Copper crystallises in fcc with a unit length of $361 \mathrm{~pm}$. What is the radius of copper atom ?
solution:
For FCC,
$r=\frac{\sqrt{2} a}{4}=\frac{a}{2 \sqrt{2}}=0.3535 a$
given $a=361 \mathrm{~pm}$
$\begin{aligned}
r & =0.3535 \times 361 \\
& =128 \mathrm{~pm}
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compounds is likely to show both Frenkel and Schottky defects in its crystalline form?
question: Which of the following compounds is likely to show both Frenkel and Schottky defects in its crystalline form?
solution:
AgBr can exhibit both Schottky and Frenkel defect.

Schottky defect in AgBr is exhibited due to the precipitation of both cations and anions. In AgBr, Ag+ ion is small in size and when removed from the lattice point, they can occupy the interstitial sites and, hence, shows both Frenkel and Schottky defects.

subject: Chemistry
q:
An atomic substance A of molar mass 12 g mol–1 has a cubic crystal structure with edge length of 300 pm. The no. of atoms present in one unit cell of A is

(Nearest integer)

Given the density of A is 3.0 g m m-1 and NAA=6.02×1023 mol-1
question:
An atomic substance A of molar mass 12 g mol–1 has a cubic crystal structure with edge length of 300 pm. The no. of atoms present in one unit cell of A is

(Nearest integer)

Given the density of A is 3.0 g m m-1 and NAA=6.02×1023 mol-1
solution:
The density of crystal can be calculated as follows,

ρ=Z×MNA×a3

ρ=density of the unit cell

Z=Effective atomic number

M=Molar mass

NA=Avogadro's number

a=Edge length of the unit cell

3=Z×126.02×1023×(3)3×10-24

Z=6.02×10-1×274=4

subject: Chemistry
q: The one that is extensively used as a piezoelectric material is:
question: The one that is extensively used as a piezoelectric material is:
solution:
Piezoelectric materials are those that produce an electric current when placed under mechanical stress.

Quartz is used as a piezoelectric material in electronic devices.

subject: Chemistry
q:
A diatomic molecule X2 has a body-centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 300 pm. The density of the molecule is 6.17 gcm-3. The number of molecules present in 200 g of X2 is :
(Avogadro constant NA=6×1023mol-1)
question:
A diatomic molecule X2 has a body-centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 300 pm. The density of the molecule is 6.17 gcm-3. The number of molecules present in 200 g of X2 is :
(Avogadro constant NA=6×1023mol-1)
solution:
For BCC z=2

d=Z×MNA× Volume 

6.17=2×M6.02×1023×3×10-83

6.17=2×M6.02×2.7

M=50

No. of mole =20050=4

No. of molecule =4 NA

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following represents the lattice structure of A0.95O containing A2+, A3+ and O2– ions?

A.

B.

C.
question:
Which of the following represents the lattice structure of A0.95O containing A2+, A3+ and O2– ions?

A.

B.

C.
solution:
Applying the electrical neutrality principle in metal deficiency defect.

One A2+ is missing then two A2+ ions are converted into A3+ ion so 0.05 A2+ ion are missing and 0.05×2  A2+ ion are converted in A3+ ion so for each O2- ion total A2+ ion 0.85 and A3+ ion is 0.1.

Hence, A satisfies the condition.
